content:
Hello there Margitta.piatri.


Going to troubleshooting mode Sometimes updates get stuck in the memory of the pc. If you just startup again and it s back,okay. If it s not then startup in troubleshoot mode.

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/troubleshoot-mode-thunderbird

To find back that profile It might be that thunderbird has chosen for an empty profile instead of the good one after update.

Open thunderbird Choose the hamburger menu. Click on help and choose > problem solving info. Seek for : about :profiles. You see now a site with more than one profile. Seek for that profile that is not the original profile. Choose for the good profile and click on it and choose default profile. Close thunderbird and restart it.

Looking for that vanished data Thunderbird only stores emails not pictures or other data. That data is often in the document map in the directory list.

Click on the pc explorer mostly the yellowed map. Choose for documents in the list on the left. If you don t see it there click on the search bar on the right to type the name or file that s missing.

content:
Wechsel in den Fehlerbehebungsmodus Manchmal bleiben Updates im Speicher des PCs hängen. Wenn du einfach wieder startest und es wieder ist, okay. Wenn nicht, starte im Fehlersuchmodus.

https://support.mozilla.org/de/kb/fehlerbehebungsmodus-thunderbird

Um dieses Profil wiederzufinden, könnte es sein, dass Thunderbird nach dem Update ein leeres Profil anstelle des guten gewählt hat.

Öffne Thunderbird. Wähle die Hamburgerkarte. Klicken Sie auf Hilfe und wählen Sie > Problemlösungsinformationen aus. Suchen Sie nach: über :p Rofiles. Sie sehen jetzt eine Seite mit mehr als einem Profil. Suchen Sie nach einem Profil, das nicht das ursprüngliche Profil ist. Wählen Sie das gute Profil, klicken Sie darauf und wählen Sie das Standardprofil. Thunderbird schließen und neu starten.

Um diese verschwundenen Daten zu suchen, speichert Thunderbird nur E-Mails, keine Bilder oder andere Daten. Diese Daten sind oft in der Dokumentenkarte in der Verzeichnisliste enthalten.

Klicke auf den PC-Explorer, hauptsächlich auf die vergilbte Karte. Wählen Sie die Dokumente in der Liste links aus. Wenn du es dort nicht findest, klicke auf die Suchleiste rechts, um den Namen oder die fehlende Datei einzugeben.

Wie erstelle ich ein Bildschirmfoto meines Problems? https://support.mozilla.org/de/kb/wie-erstelle-ich-ein-bildschirmfoto-meines-problems

content:
Here's a fairly easy workaround *IF* you're comfortable with the command line, and if your VCF files are all in one folder. This uses a DOS command, but there are equivalents for UNIX/Linux and, I presume, Macs.

Open up a CMD/terminal session in the containing folder. At the command line type:

copy *.vcf AllAddresses.txt

"AllAddresses" is an arbitrary name, it can be whatever you want, but the file MUST NOT have a .VCF extension. When you run that command it will concatenate all the VCF files onto one big file. When it's done, and it should just take a second, then rename that file from .TXT to .VCF. This file is now suitable for importing into your Thunderbird address book.

[later edit]... See my post below - the correctly command should be:

copy /b *.vcf AllAddresses.txt
creator: rednoise

content: Thank you, Lin. I noticed a similar solution posted a few years back, but I was uncomfortable with the approach. You clarified it. Thanks. :)
creator: davidsk

content:
I just realized that I made one important mistake! I was thinking that all VCF files were straight ASCII text, but apparently not, apparently they can have some non-ASCII characters in them. Therefore, to make a valid "Big VCF" file you need to use the command line:

copy /b *.vcf AllAddresses.txt

The "/b" switch tells the copy command to treat the files as binary. If you don't do that, Thunderbird may give you an error message, and while it may still import the file, there might be some corruption in there (although I couldn't find any.)

Oops!

content:
Do you have an Anti-virus which is scanning all outgoing mail and is also allowed to scan any opened file in your Thunderbird profile folder ? They get updated very frequently and cause no end of problems interrupting processes etc. As a heads up, never allow any Anti-Virus to auto fix anything because they have no understanding that an mbox file contains more than one email, so a fix could mean complete loss of everything in that file.

Suggestion: Access Anti-Virus and stop it scanning outgoing mail. Also set up an exception to not scan anything in your Thunderbird profile folder. If you downloaded and installed Thunderbird from the official website then it would be in this location:

  • C://Users/username/Appdata/Roaming/Thunderbird

If you installed from something like 'Windows App Store' then Windows will shove the profile location elsewhere which is a nuisance -

  • C:\Users\username\AppData\Local\packages\MozillaThunderbird.MZLA_h5892qc0xkpca\LocalCache\Roaming\Thunderbird

content:
Steps to Create a Mailing List

  1. Open an Address Book tab: Click the Address Book icon in the Spaces Toolbar at the top left or press Ctrl + Shift + B
  2. New Mailing List: Click the "New Mailing List" icon to the right of the "New Contact" button
  3. Name the List: Give your list a recognizable name
  4. Add Members: Start typing the names or email addresses of your contacts into the list rows. Thunderbird will autocomplete entries from your existing address book.
  5. Save: Click OK to finish.

content:
https://kb.mozillazine.org/Nstmp_folders

Please try this first:

  • Go into 'Offline' mode first
  • Then compact folders - you could try compacting on specific folders as mentioned below.
  • Do not attempt to view any folders or emails whilst this occurs.
  • Do not exit Thunderbird.

I notice you mention 'Gmail' - do you have an 'All Mail' folder?

Try to compact on specific folders. The most common files that need compacting are those that get a lot of deletions or moves. examples 'Inbox', 'Drafts', 'Spam', 'Trash' and 'All Mail'

When completed:

  • Right click on 'Drafts' for one account and select 'compact'.

etc


Look in profile folders to see if there are any nstmp files.

  • In 'Help' > 'Troubleshooting Information'
  • Under 'Application Basics' section - half way is 'Profile Folder' - click on 'Open Folder'
  • It opens in a new window
  • click on 'ImapMail' folder to see contents
  • click on a mail account folder to see contents

Do you see any nstmp files ?

content:
I figured out how to turn on logging of "compact" events. I've browsed those logs and reviewed the (admittedly old) github version of that code (because it was too difficult for me to set up mercurial to fetch newer code). I now understand that the "992MB" number is across all servers and folders that TB is managing. The logs don't give me a lot of details about how that 991..993MB number is calculated. The code mentions something about summing up the values found for both local and online as well, but I don't know what that is. (That (admittedly old) code seems to use global variables in a way that might result in a miscalculation if certain (unfamiliar to me) criteria are met. But I don't don't think that's this problem.) I'm hypothesizing that there is something that I've not identified that the code is seeing that consistently boosts the estimated saving up to about 991MB and the code might find another 1..2 MB of actual expungedBytes. The result is an estimate of expunged 991MB..993MB. According to the logs the subsequent compaction does eliminate those extra 1..2 MB that it found.

I'm also running into a bit of trouble with the logs because they seem to stop in the middle of a logging line as if the logging is not fully flushed to disk. You might know a way to work around that. With that being said, I don't think the problem is hidden by these unflushed log lines.

I don't know what online folders vs local folders means in the context of compaction. Perhaps only one of them is being compacted but the other is the one that is responsible for boosting the estimate up by 991MB?

I'll try to read the code again tomorrow, but suggestions are welcome.
creator: trewtus+200514ffox

content:
Online (actually offline) folders refers to messages which have been downloaded for offline use from imap accounts.

Your 991MB is indeed a number across all accounts. In theory, the variable gets incremented as messages are deleted, and decremented as compacts occur on individual folders. In other words, it is not derived by summing all folders each time a compact might happen.

Do you have filters on pop accounts? Are your accounts all imap or all pop?

"Can not quite get email to work" is too vague to troubleshoot from. Specific detailed description of what you did and what error messages you see is necessary. But I'll guess...

Method is the same for all platforms. If it's not working with the "experimental" Account Hub feature, turn it off and configure manually. That feature is found at Settings | General | Account Hub>. I can't verify them, but give them a try I found them at

https://help.earthlink.net/portal/en/kb/articles/email-server-settings

EarthLink POP3 Settings

Incoming POP3 settings are unique to each EarthLink domain. The format is pop.(domain name). The outgoing server is the same regardless of domain. 

   Pop Incoming Server: pop.earthlink.net (pop.mindspring.com, pop.peoplepc.com, etc)    Incoming Port Number: 995    Incoming Security Setting: SSL/TLS    Authentication is Required: Use your full email address and password. 
   Outgoing Server: smtpauth.earthlink.net (same for all EarthLink domains)    Outgoing Port Number: 587    Outgoing Security Setting: STARTTLS    Authentication is Required: Use your full email address and password.
